title: Find and fix the median bug
prompt: The function below is meant to return the median of a list of numbers, but it is
buggy. Find the bug and return a corrected version.
- For an odd-length list, the median is the middle element.
- For an even-length list, the median is the average of the two middle
elements (a float, e.g. the median of [1, 2, 3, 4] is 2.5).
You may assume the list is non-empty.
Respond with a SINGLE Python code block (```python ... ```) defining the corrected
median function and nothing else. Include no explanation outside the code block.
difficulty_rationale: Requires recognizing that the given code silently mishandles even-length input (returns the upper-middle element, never averages). The fix is short but the diagnosis is the work. Hidden even-length asserts ([1,10]->5.5) fail any un-fixed or off-by-one variant. Expected to separate low (may miss the even case) from high.
